description_long: This object consists of two long, straight-bladed swords with a metallic, slightly mottled texture, suggesting forged steel. Each blade is rectangular with a pointed tip, one of which is slightly angled, and both have a flat, broad profile. The handles are cylindrical and wrapped in a brown material, likely wood or leather, capped with a metallic pommel. A rectangular crossguard separates the blade from the handle, providing hand protection. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The swords appear robust and functional, suitable for practical use rather than ceremonial display.
description: A pair of straight, broad-bladed swords with brown cylindrical handles and metallic crossguards, featuring a utilitarian, unadorned design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of both swords, highlighting the straight blades, brown handles, and metallic crossguards.
description_view_1: Blade-forward view emphasizing the broad, flat surfaces of the blades and the rectangular crossguards.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the length and profile of the blades, with clear visibility of the handle and pommel details.
description_view_3: Handle-forward view focusing on the cylindrical handles and the alignment of the blades, with the crossguards visible from the rear.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, brown, likely wood or leather-wrapped, metallic pommel
